provenance.pin_detail: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

privilege_notes: runs fetched package code at launch

findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'tavily' is fetched and executed at launch via 'npx' with auto-accept flag(s) -y; package spec: tavily-mcp.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable
findings.detail: Server 'tavily' package spec 'tavily-mcp' is unpinned (pin strength: none): no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now).
findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

evidence.reason: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

findings.title: Server requires sensitive credentials
findings.detail: Server 'tavily' requires credential-like env keys: TAVILY_API_KEY.
findings.recommendation: Confirm each credential is scoped to least privilege and rotatable. Note which tools can exfiltrate it.

tools.name: tavily_search
tools.description: Search the web for current information on any topic. Use for news, facts, or data beyond your knowledge cutoff. Returns snippets and source URLs.

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.

candidate_capabilities.title: Direct database / data-store access
candidate_capabilities.rationale: Raw query access bypasses application-level authorization and tenant scoping; injection in a tool parameter reaches the data directly.

tools.name: tavily_extract
tools.description: Extract content from URLs. Returns raw page content in markdown or text format.

candidate_capabilities.title: Direct database / data-store access
candidate_capabilities.rationale: Raw query access bypasses application-level authorization and tenant scoping; injection in a tool parameter reaches the data directly.

tools.name: tavily_crawl
tools.description: Crawl a website starting from a URL. Extracts content from pages with configurable depth and breadth.

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.

candidate_capabilities.title: Privilege / permission change
candidate_capabilities.rationale: Tools that alter permissions or escalate privilege can expand their own blast radius and persist access.

data_categories.label: Public / aggregate data
data_categories.rationale: Already public or non-identifying — minimal exposure.

tools.name: tavily_map
tools.description: Map a website's structure. Returns a list of URLs found starting from the base URL.

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.

candidate_capabilities.title: Privilege / permission change
candidate_capabilities.rationale: Tools that alter permissions or escalate privilege can expand their own blast radius and persist access.

data_categories.label: Public / aggregate data
data_categories.rationale: Already public or non-identifying — minimal exposure.

tools.name: tavily_research
tools.description: Perform comprehensive research on a given topic or question. Use this tool when you need to gather information from multiple sources to answer a question or complete a task. Returns a detailed response based on the research findings. Rate limit: 20 requests per minute.

data_categories.label: Project / task / status metadata
data_categories.rationale: Names, statuses, and structure of work — identifying but not the underlying content.

toxic_combinations.title: Read-then-send exfiltration chain
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can both read secrets/credentials and make outbound network calls — a complete path to exfiltrate them to an attacker-chosen host.
